entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Cereal crop used in rotation with sunflower in rainfed semi-arid tropical Alfisols

entities.name: SUNFLOWER
entities.description: Oilseed crop used in rotation with sorghum in rainfed semi-arid tropical Alfisols

entities.name: HORSE GRAM
entities.description: Legume used for biomass incorporation to assess effects on soil carbon dioxide emissions and quality

entities.name: CARBON DIOXIDE EMISSION
entities.description: The release of CO2 from soil, used as an indicator of soil respiration and greenhouse gas emissions

entities.name: SOIL QUALITY
entities.description: The capacity of soil to function within ecosystem boundaries to sustain plant and animal productivity

entities.name: ALFISOL
entities.description: A type of soil characterized by high base saturation and an argillic horizon, found in dryland semi-arid tropics

entities.name: NITROGEN FERTILIZER
entities.description: Application of nitrogen-based nutrients to improve soil fertility

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS FERTILIZER
entities.description: Application of phosphorus-based nutrients to improve soil fertility

entities.name: LEGUME BIOMASS INCORPORATION
entities.description: The practice of adding legume plant matter into the soil to improve organic content and fertility

entities.name: SOIL MOISTURE
entities.description: The amount of water held in the soil

entities.name: SOIL TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The temperature of the soil at a specific depth

entities.name: SOIL DEHYDROGENASES
entities.description: Enzymes that serve as indicators of microbial activity in the soil

entities.name: MICROBIAL BIOMASS CARBON
entities.description: The carbon contained within the living microbial biomass of the soil

entities.name: KHARIF PERIOD
entities.description: The monsoon cropping season in South Asia

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: A climatic region characterized by low and irregular rainfall and high temperatures

entities.name: GREENHOUSE GAS EMISSIONS
entities.description: Gases that trap heat in the atmosphere, including carbon dioxide

relationships.description: Increases rates of emission over fallow conditions during Kharif and legume periods
Increases the content of microbial biomass carbon
Found to be the greatest significant variable correlated with CO2 emission
Is a component of greenhouse gas emissions
Soil type found in semi-arid tropical regions
